chunks.text:
# Manufacturing Business Glossary

This glossary defines the core business concepts and terminology used in the manufacturing supply chain domain. It covers product structures, materials management, production workflows, and quality assurance processes.

## Product

A Product represents any manufactured item in the system, including finished goods ready for sale, sub-assemblies used in production, and individual components. Products are organized in a hierarchical structure where complex product... [TRUNCATED]

## Warehouse

A Warehouse represents a physical storage location where materials, components, and finished goods are stored. Warehouses have defined capacity limits, geographic location information, and are managed by designated managers. Warehouses serve as nodes in the supply chain network where inventory is held, shipments are received, and products are staged for production or distribution.

**Key Attributes:**
- Unique warehouse identifier
- Warehouse name and location
- Address, city, and ... [TRUNCATED]

## QualityControl

Quality Control (QC) represents inspection and testing activities performed on materials, in-process production, or finished goods. QC records track the type of inspection (incoming, in-process, final), inspection date, inspector, test results (pass, fail, conditional), defect counts, and notes. QC activities are associated with specific production batches and may reference technical specifications being tested.

**Key Attributes:**
- Unique QC identifier
- Batch reference (pr... [TRUNCATED]

# Manufacturing Data Dictionary

This data dictionary documents the database schema for the manufacturing supply chain system. It includes all tables, columns, data types, constraints, and relationships. Special attention is given to self-referential relationships that support hierarchical product structures.

## product

Stores all manufactured items including finished goods, assemblies, and components. Supports hierarchical product relationships via self-referencing foreign key.

| Column | Da... [TRUNCATED]

## bom

Bill of Materials defining product composition and hierarchical structure. Supports recursive relationships for multi-level assemblies.

| Column | Data Type | Constraints | Description |
|--------|-----------|-------------|-------------|
| bom_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique BOM identifier |
| parent_product_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → product(product_id) | Parent product (what is being built) |
| component_product_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → produc... [TRUNCATED]

## warehouse

Stores physical storage location information for inventory management.

| Column | Data Type | Constraints | Description |
|--------|-----------|-------------|-------------|
| warehouse_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique warehouse identifier |
| warehouse_name | VARCHAR(100) | NOT NULL | Warehouse name |
| address | VARCHAR(200) | | Street address |
| city | VARCHAR(50) | | City location |
| state | VARCHAR(50) | | State or province |
| capacity | INT | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0, CH... [TRUNCATED]

## work_order

Represents production jobs for manufacturing products with hierarchical work order support.

| Column | Data Type | Constraints | Description |
|--------|-----------|-------------|-------------|
| work_order_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique work order identifier |
| product_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → product(product_id) | Product to manufacture |
| parent_work_order | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → work_order(work_order_id) | Parent work order for decomposition... [TRUNCATED]

## specification

Defines technical requirements and acceptance criteria for materials and products.

| Column | Data Type | Constraints | Description |
|--------|-----------|-------------|-------------|
| specification_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique specification identifier |
| specification_name | VARCHAR(100) | NOT NULL | Specification name |
| version | VARCHAR(10) | NOT NULL | Specification version |
| effective_date | DATE | NOT NULL | Effective date |
| spec_type | spec_type_t | ... [TRUNCATED]

## shipment

Tracks material transfers between warehouses, suppliers, and customers.

| Column | Data Type | Constraints | Description |
|--------|-----------|-------------|-------------|
| shipment_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique shipment identifier |
| shipment_type | shipment_type_t | NOT NULL | INBOUND, OUTBOUND, INTERNAL |
| warehouse_id | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → warehouse(warehouse_id) | Warehouse reference |
| supplier_id | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → supplier(supplier_id) | S... [TRUNCATED]

entities_post_resolution.name: Work Order
entities_post_resolution.definition: A Work Order is a manufacturing instruction that represents a production job or manufacturing run to produce a specified quantity of a product, and can be decomposed into child work orders in a hierarchical structure.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
Self-Referential Relationship:
- `parent_work_order` references `work_order_id` — enables work order decomposition
- A large production job can be broken into child work orders | A Work Order represents a production job or manufacturing run to produce a specified quantity of a product.

entities_post_resolution.name: Product
entities_post_resolution.definition: A product is a manufactured item (finished goods, sub-assemblies, or components) tracked in the system, with attributes such as name, type classification, and production data, and capable of being composed into bill-of-materials.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
# Product

A Product represents any manufactured item in the system, including finished goods ready for sale, sub-assemblies used in production, and individual components. | Each product has a unique identifier, name, type classification, base cost, and lead time for procurement or production. | Products can be active or inactive based on current manufacturing requirements. | Hierarchical relationship via parent_product_id | A Product can have multiple child Products (via BOM hierarchy) | A Product can reference a parent Product (for multi-level assemblies) | Products are used in Work Orders for production | Products appear in Shipments (outbound) | Products are stored in Inventory records | Work Orders produce Products | - A product can contain other products as components via BOM | Stores all manufactured items including finished goods, assemblies, and components. | Supports hierarchical product relationships via self-referencing foreign key. | Self-Referential Structure: - Both `par

entities_post_resolution.name: Component
entities_post_resolution.definition: A component is an atomic raw material or purchased part used in manufacturing, categorized by a component category and identified by a component name, and procured from external suppliers.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: A Component represents atomic raw materials or purchased parts that cannot be further broken down into simpler manufactured items. | Components are the building blocks used in manufacturing and are procured from external suppliers. | Each component has a standard cost, unit of measure, and may be associated with technical specifications that define quality requirements. | and may be associated with technical specifications that define quality requirements. | Components are supplied by multiple Suppliers (many-to-many) | Components have Inventory records tracking stock levels | Suppliers provide multiple Components (many-to-many via component_supplier) | Stores atomic raw materials and purchased parts that are procured from external suppliers. | - One-to-many with inventory | - Optional relationship with specification | | component_category | VARCHAR(50) | | Component category classification | | | component_name | VARCHAR(100) | NOT NULL | Component name |

entities_post_resolution.name: Inventory
entities_post_resolution.definition: Inventory represents the stock levels of components and products across warehouses, with inventory records that track quantities on hand, quantities reserved for pending orders, and reorder thresholds.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
Inventory represents the actual stock levels of components and products stored in specific warehouses. Each inventory record tracks the quantity on hand, quantity reserved for pending orders, and reorder thresholds to trigger procurement. | Each inventory record tracks the quantity on hand, quantity reserved for pending orders, and reorder thresholds to trigger procurement. | Last restock date | Inventory belongs to a Warehouse | Inventory tracks either a Component or Product | Work Orders consume Inventory (components) | Products are stored in Inventory records | Components have Inventory records tracking stock levels | Warehouses contain multiple Inventory records | - One-to-many with inventory | Relationships:
- One-to-many with inventory | Tracks stock levels for components and products at specific warehouse locations. | inventory_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique inventory identifier | warehouse_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → warehouse(warehouse_id) | Warehouse l

entities_post_resolution.name: Shipment Type
entities_post_resolution.definition: Shipment is the record of the physical transfer of materials between locations (inbound, outbound, or internal) tracked with a type such as INBOUND, OUTBOUND, or INTERNAL.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
| shipment_type | shipment_type_t | NOT NULL | INBOUND, OUTBOUND, INTERNAL | | A Shipment represents the physical transfer of materials between warehouses, from suppliers to warehouses (inbound), or from warehouses to customers (outbound). | Key Attributes:
- Type: INBOUND, OUTBOUND, INTERNAL | Shipments connect Warehouses, Suppliers, and Customers | Inbound Shipments reference Suppliers | Customer reference (for outbound) | Shipments update Inventory levels | Shipments fulfill Work Order outputs | Products appear in Shipments (outbound) | Warehouses receive and ship Shipments | Relationships:
- One-to-many with shipment | ## shipment

Tracks material transfers between warehouses, suppliers, and customers.

entities_post_resolution.name: Technical Specification
entities_post_resolution.definition: A technical specification is a formal document that defines the technical requirements, standards, and acceptance criteria governing materials, products, or processes.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
A Specification defines technical requirements, standards, and acceptance criteria for materials or products. | ## specification

Defines technical requirements and acceptance criteria for materials and products. | QC tests against Specifications | Specifications include version numbers, effective dates, specification types (dimensional, material, performance), critical parameters to test, and acceptable value ranges with units of measure. | Specifications ensure manufactured products meet engineering and customer requirements. | | specification_id | VARCHAR(20) | | Technical specification reference | | - Optional relationship with specification | and may be associated with technical specifications that define quality requirements.

entities_post_resolution.name: Unique Parent Product
entities_post_resolution.definition: A unique constraint on the combination of component_id and supplier_id to prevent duplicate supplier associations for a given component.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Constraints: - `uq_bom_structure` UNIQUE (parent_product_id, component_product_id) — prevents duplicate entries | Constraints: - `uq_component_supplier` UNIQUE (component_id, supplier_id) — prevents duplicate supplier associations

entities_post_resolution.name: Warehouse
entities_post_resolution.definition: A warehouse is a physical storage location used for inventory management, characterized by capacity, geographic location, and a unique identifier, and referenced by IDs in related systems and processes (e.g., shipments, work orders, and production locations).
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
Warehouses have defined capacity limits, geographic location information, and are managed by designated managers. | Warehouses contain multiple Inventory records | Warehouses receive and ship Shipments | Warehouses are linked to Work Orders for production staging | Warehouses store produced Batches | Inventory belongs to a Warehouse | Work Orders are associated with Warehouses | Shipments connect Warehouses, Suppliers, and Customers | Stores physical storage location information for inventory management. | Relationships:
- One-to-many with inventory | Relationships:
- One-to-many with shipment | Relationships:
- One-to-many with work_order | Relationships:
- One-to-many with batch | | warehouse_id | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → warehouse(warehouse_id) | Warehouse reference | | warehouse_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → warehouse(warehouse_id) | Warehouse location | | warehouse_id | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → warehouse(warehouse_id) | Production location | | Indexes:
- `idx_sh

entities_post_resolution.name: Manufactured Item
entities_post_resolution.definition: A manufactured item refers to any product produced within the system, including finished goods, sub-assemblies, assemblies, and components.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
# Product

A Product represents any manufactured item in the system, including finished goods ready for sale, sub-assemblies used in production, and individual components. | Stores all manufactured items including finished goods, assemblies, and components.

entities_post_resolution.name: Component(Component Id)
entities_post_resolution.definition: A unique identifier for a single component-to-supplier relationship, i.e., a row in the component_supplier junction table recording which supplier supplies a given component.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: component_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → component(component_id) | Component reference | component_id | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → component(component_id) | Component reference (exclusive with product_id) | | component_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique component identifier | | Indexes: - `idx_cs_component` on component_id | component_product_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → product(product_id) | Component product (what goes into it) | - Many-to-many with supplier via component_supplier junction table | component_supplier_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique relationship identifier

entities_post_resolution.name: Product
entities_post_resolution.definition: A Product entity in the product catalog, identified by product_id, which can reference a parent Product via parent_product_id to support hierarchical assemblies.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
# Product

A Product represents any manufactured item in the system, including finished goods ready for sale, sub-assemblies used in production, and individual components. | Each product has a unique identifier, name, type classification, base cost, and lead time for procurement or production. | Products can be active or inactive based on current manufacturing requirements. | Hierarchical relationship via parent_product_id | A Product can have multiple child Products (via BOM hierarchy) | A Product can reference a parent Product (for multi-level assemblies) | Products are used in Work Orders for production | Products appear in Shipments (outbound) | Products are stored in Inventory records | Work Orders produce Products | - A product can contain other products as components via BOM | Stores all manufactured items including finished goods, assemblies, and components. | Supports hierarchical product relationships via self-referencing foreign key. | Self-Referential Structure: - Both `par

entities_post_resolution.name: Supplier(Supplier Id)
entities_post_resolution.definition: Name attribute of a supplier entity.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: supplier_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → supplier(supplier_id) | Supplier reference | | supplier_id | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → supplier(supplier_id) | Supplier (inbound only) | | - Many-to-many with supplier via component_supplier junction table | supplier_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique supplier identifier | Indexes: - `idx_cs_supplier` on supplier_id | supplier_name | VARCHAR(100) | NOT NULL | Supplier name

entities_post_resolution.name: Batch
entities_post_resolution.definition: A Batch is a production lot or run of a product, tracked for traceability and inventory management, identified by a batch_id and subject to quality control.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
A Batch represents a specific production lot or run of a product, tracking all units produced together in a single manufacturing cycle. | ## batch

Tracks production lots for traceability and inventory management. | QC results determine Batch status | Warehouses store produced Batches | QC inspections are performed on Batches | Batches record the product produced, production date, quantity produced, warehouse location, expiry date (if applicable), and quality control status. | Batches record ... quality control status. | Batches enable traceability for quality issues and inventory management for time-sensitive or lot-controlled products. | Relationships:
- One-to-many with batch | | batch_id | VARCHAR(20) | | Production batch reference |

entities_post_resolution.name: Contact Email Address
entities_post_resolution.definition: A field representing the user's email address used for electronic communication.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: contact_email | VARCHAR(100) | | Contact email address | contact_phone | VARCHAR(20) | | Contact phone number

entities_post_resolution.name: Hierarchical Product Structures
entities_post_resolution.definition: Represents the hierarchical arrangement of products in a catalog or bill of materials, modeling self-referential parent-child relationships and the depth of each item in the product hierarchy.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: bom_level | INT | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0, CHECK >= 0 | Depth in product hierarchy | Supports hierarchical product relationships via self-referencing foreign key. | Special attention is given to self-referential relationships that support hierarchical product structures. | Hierarchical relationship via parent_product_id

entities_post_resolution.name: Data Flow Direction
entities_post_resolution.definition: An attribute describing the direction of data movement (INBOUND, OUTBOUND, INTERNAL) within a system or data governance context.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
Key Attributes:
- Type: INBOUND, OUTBOUND, INTERNAL

entities_post_resolution.name: Last Restock Date
entities_post_resolution.definition: The date/time indicating the most recent restock of inventory.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: last_restock_date | DATE | | Last restock timestamp | Last restock date

entities_post_resolution.name: Performance Rating
entities_post_resolution.definition: A numeric performance rating value on a 1.0–5.0 decimal scale (default 3.00, not null) used to rate performance.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: rating | DECIMAL(3,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 3.00, CHECK 1.0-5.0 | Performance rating

entities_post_resolution.name: Procurement Or Production
entities_post_resolution.definition: The number of days required to procure or manufacture a product, i.e., the lead time from ordering to receipt or readiness.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | lead_time_days | INT | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0, CHECK >= 0 | Procurement or production lead time | | lead_time_days | INT | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0 | Procurement lead time | Each product has a unique identifier, name, type classification, base cost, and lead time for procurement or production.

entities_post_resolution.name: Qc Inspections
entities_post_resolution.definition: QC records are the documentation that captures details of quality-control inspections, including type of inspection (incoming, in-process, final), inspection date, inspector, test results, defect counts, and notes.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: QC inspections are performed on Batches | QC records track the type of inspection (incoming, in-process, final), inspection date, inspector, test results (pass, fail, conditional), defect counts, and notes. | QC results determine Batch status | QC tests against Specifications

entities_post_resolution.name: Unit Of Measure
entities_post_resolution.definition: A unit used to express the quantity of a product or item (e.g., EA, KG, M, L) in a business data model.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | unit_of_measure | VARCHAR(10) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 'EA' | Unit of measure (EA, KG, M, L, etc.) | | unit_of_measure | VARCHAR(10) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 'EA' | Unit of measure

entities_post_resolution.name: Work Order
entities_post_resolution.definition: A Work Order is a production job in manufacturing used to plan, execute, and track the manufacture of a specified quantity of a product, including possible decomposition into sub-orders.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
Shipments fulfill Work Order outputs | Products are used in Work Orders for production | Warehouses are linked to Work Orders for production staging | A Work Order represents a production job or manufacturing run to produce a specified quantity of a product. | Work Orders track planned versus completed quantities, status through the production lifecycle, priority levels, and planned dates. | Work Orders can have hierarchical relationships where a parent work order may decompose into child work orders for different production stages or sub-assemblies. | Work Orders produce Products | Work Orders are associated with Warehouses | Work Orders consume Inventory (components) | Work Orders produce output for Shipment | The `work_order` table's `parent_work_order` column references `work_order_id`, enabling: | Relationships:
- One-to-many with work_order | ## work_order

Represents production jobs for manufacturing products with hierarchical work order support. | Self-Referential Relationship:

entities_post_resolution.name: Is Active
entities_post_resolution.definition: A boolean Yes/No flag indicating whether the entity is currently active (true) or inactive (false), typically stored as a yes/no field with a default of yes.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | is_active | yes_no_t | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 'Y' | Active status flag | | Products can be active or inactive based on current manufacturing requirements.

entities_post_resolution.name: Ensures Actual Arrival
entities_post_resolution.definition: Shipment date — the date on which the shipment is dispatched, serving as the base reference for calculating estimated and actual arrival dates.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: - `chk_shipment_dates` — ensures actual_arrival >= ship_date | | actual_arrival | DATE | | Actual arrival date | | | estimated_arrival | DATE | | Estimated arrival date | | | ship_date | DATE | NOT NULL | Shipment date | | - `idx_shipment_dates` on (ship_date, estimated_arrival)

entities_post_resolution.name: Atomic Raw Materials
entities_post_resolution.definition: Atomic raw materials and purchased parts are indivisible items sourced from external suppliers that are stored as inventory in manufacturing operations.
synonyms: atomic raw materials or purchased parts
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Stores atomic raw materials and purchased parts that are procured from external suppliers. | A Component represents atomic raw materials or purchased parts that cannot be further broken down into simpler manufactured items.

entities_post_resolution.name: Standard Procurement Cost
entities_post_resolution.definition: Base manufacturing cost is the baseline cost incurred to manufacture a component or product, used as a cost reference in manufacturing budgeting and costing.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | standard_cost | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK >= 0 | Standard procurement cost | | | base_cost | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK >= 0 | Base manufacturing cost | | Each component has a standard cost, unit of measure, and may be associated with technical specifications that define quality requirements.

entities_post_resolution.name: Physical Storage Location Information
entities_post_resolution.definition: Information about the physical storage locations used to store and manage inventory within facilities, including bin and shelf locations.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Stores physical storage location information for inventory management. | Warehouses have defined capacity limits, geographic location information, and are managed by designated managers.

entities_post_resolution.name: Database Index
entities_post_resolution.definition: A database index is a data structure that speeds up query performance by maintaining an ordered representation of a column or set of columns in a table.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
Indexes: - `idx_cs_component` on component_id | Indexes: - `idx_cs_supplier` on supplier_id | - `idx_shipment_dates` on (ship_date, estimated_arrival) | - `idx_shipment_status` on status | - `idx_shipment_supplier` on supplier_id (WHERE NOT NULL) | Indexes:
- `idx_shipment_warehouse` on warehouse_id

entities_post_resolution.name: Type Of Inspection
entities_post_resolution.definition: An attribute describing the category of an inspection (e.g., incoming, in-process, final) in quality control records.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: QC records track the type of inspection (incoming, in-process, final), inspection date, inspector, test results (pass, fail, conditional), defect counts, and notes.

entities_post_resolution.name: Manufacturing Workflow Steps
entities_post_resolution.definition: The defined sequence of manufacturing workflow steps and operations that specify how a product is produced, used for production planning, scheduling, capacity planning, and cost calculation.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
## route

Defines manufacturing workflow steps and operations for product production. | Routes collectively define the manufacturing workflow and are used for production scheduling, capacity planning, and cost calculation.

entities_post_resolution.definition: A unique identifier for a quality control (QC) record.

| qc_date | DATE | NOT NULL | Inspection date | | ## quality_control

| qc_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique QC identifier | | | qc_type | qc_type_t | NOT NULL | INCOMING, IN_PROCESS, FINAL |

entities_post_resolution.name: Quantity Reserved
entities_post_resolution.definition: The quantity_on_hand is the available stock quantity of an item currently in inventory that can be allocated to fulfill orders.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Each inventory record tracks the quantity on hand, quantity reserved for pending orders, and reorder thresholds to trigger procurement. | quantity | DECIMAL(10,3) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 1.000, CHECK > 0 | Required quantity | Inventory represents the actual stock levels of components and products stored in specific warehouses. Each inventory record tracks the quantity on hand, quantity reserved for pending orders, and reorder thresholds to trigger procurement. | quantity_on_hand | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK >= 0 | Available quantity | quantity_reserved | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK >= 0 | Reserved quantity

entities_post_resolution.name: Recursive Relationships
entities_post_resolution.definition: A relationship type in a Bill of Materials that structures components in a recursive, parent–child hierarchy to support multi-level assemblies.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Bill of Materials defining product composition and hierarchical structure. Supports recursive relationships for multi-level assemblies. | Special attention is given to self-referential relationships that support hierarchical product structures.

entities_post_resolution.definition: The numeric value indicating the stock level at which a reorder is triggered to replenish inventory.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: reorder_threshold | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00 | Reorder trigger level | Each inventory record tracks the quantity on hand, quantity reserved for pending orders, and reorder thresholds to trigger procurement.

entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Specifications include version numbers, effective dates, specification types (dimensional, material, performance), critical parameters to test, and acceptable value ranges with units of measure.

tables_enriched.name: Product
tables_enriched.description: Stores information about products, including hierarchical relationships between products, pricing, availability, and lifecycle status.

tables_enriched.name: Component
tables_enriched.description: Stores data about individual components, including identifiers, names, categories, measurement units, standard cost, and related specification reference.

tables_enriched.name: BOM (Bill of Materials)
tables_enriched.description: Stores the Bill of Materials relationships: which parent product uses which component product, the quantity and unit of measure, and BOM hierarchy level with an optional flag.

tables_enriched.name: Supplier
tables_enriched.description: Stores information about suppliers, including contact details, quality rating, and whether the supplier is a preferred partner.

tables_enriched.name: Warehouse
tables_enriched.description: Stores information about each warehouse, including location, capacity, and the assigned manager.

tables_enriched.name: Inventory
tables_enriched.description: Stores current stock levels and restocking parameters for inventory items across warehouses, including on-hand quantities, reserved quantities, and restock thresholds.

tables_enriched.name: Work Order
tables_enriched.description: Stores work order records for product fulfillment, including quantities, status, priority, planned schedule, and associated warehouse and parent order relationships.

tables_enriched.name: Shipment
tables_enriched.description: Stores information about shipments, including identifiers, participants (warehouse, supplier, and customer), scheduling dates, and current status.

tables_enriched.name: Quality Control
tables_enriched.description: Stores records of quality control checks for product batches, including the date, type of QC, inspector, outcome, any defects found, and accompanying notes.

tables_enriched.name: Specification
tables_enriched.description: Stores specifications and their applicable values, including type, defining parameters, and valid value ranges for products or processes.

tables_enriched.name: Batch
tables_enriched.description: Stores records of product batches including where they are produced, quantities, warehouse location, expiry, and QC status.

tables_enriched.name: Route
tables_enriched.description: Stores the workflow routes for each product, listing the sequence of operations, the work centers involved, and the time required for setup and processing.

tables_enriched.name: Component Supplier
tables_enriched.description: Stores relationships between components and suppliers, including whether a supplier is preferred and the lead time and price for each component from that supplier.
